title: Kendall Square urban renewal : environmental impact statement.

schema.page_numbers: ESTIMATED from char offsets at 2500 chars/page.
schema.merge_rule: One row per (entity, stance) — stance changes produce separate rows.
schema.cost_note: USD costs are ESTIMATES from settings.PRICES_USD_PER_M; verify against AWS Bedrock invoice.

entries.role: Municipal legislative body, City of Cambridge
entries.summary_quote: On October 9, 1974, the City Council passed a resolution which endorsed the Neighborhood Plan in principle and called upon the City Manager, the Redevelopment Authority and City Staff to move forward as rapidly as possible to attract development to the site which would provide jobs, a decent and human environment and a substantial increase in the City's tax base.

mentions.quote: The City Council order of October 9, 1974 designated the Neighborhood Plan as the 'Preferred Plan of the City Council' and ordered that the Task Force and City Manager's Plans be submitted to HUD as alternatives.
mentions.stance_basis: Designated Neighborhood Plan as preferred and unanimously approved Amendment No. 1 to the Urban Renewal Plan

mentions.quote: On July 2, 1973, the Cambridge City Council passed a City order specifying that the Kendall Square site be used to create and provide maximum blue collar and nonprofessional white collar jobs for Cambridge residents.
mentions.stance_basis: Passed City order directing Kendall Square site development for job creation for Cambridge residents
mentions.role_as_written: Municipal legislative body, City of Cambridge

mentions.quote: On October 9, 1974, the City Council passed a resolution which endorsed the Neighborhood Plan in principle and called upon the City Manager, the Redevelopment Authority and City Staff to move forward as rapidly as possible to attract development to the site which would provide jobs, a decent and human environment and a substantial increase in the City's tax base.
mentions.stance_basis: Endorsed the Neighborhood Plan and called for rapid development of the Kendall Square site
mentions.role_as_written: Municipal legislative body, City of Cambridge

entries.summary_quote: Local planners believe that rent control is a significant force in preserving the existing socioeconomic makeup of the neighborhoods in Cambridge and has reduced the out-migration of low income workers.

mentions.quote: Local planners believe that rent control is a significant force in preserving the existing socioeconomic makeup of the neighborhoods in Cambridge and has reduced the out-migration of low income workers.
mentions.stance_basis: Expressed view on rent control's role in neighborhood preservation, not directly on the project

mentions.quote: The shortage of recreational and open space in Cambridge is considered to be a problem by local planners.
mentions.stance_basis: Identified open space shortage as a problem relevant to project context

rubric_results.check: Summary quote verbatim in cited pages
rubric_results.note: The exact sentence appears on page II-3-14: 'Local planners believe that rent control is a significant force in preserving the existing socioeconomic makeup of the neighborhoods in Cambridge and has reduced the out-migration of low income workers.'

rubric_results.check: Attribution links entity to stance
rubric_results.note: The narrator sentence names 'Local planners' as the subject holding this belief, consistent with paraphrased attribution mode.

rubric_results.check: Stance label matches evidence
rubric_results.note: The statement expresses a positive view of rent control's effects; 'neutral' does not fit. The planners are expressing a favorable/supportive position toward rent control, which would be 'in_favor' or at minimum 'conditional'. However, the stance may be neutral with respect to the Kendall Square project itself rather than rent control policy.

rubric_results.check: Entity name well-formed
rubric_results.note: 'local planners' is a generic group descriptor used in the source text; acceptable as an entity label.

rubric_results.check: Role description supported by cited text
rubric_results.note: The text references 'local planners' in the context of Cambridge housing and open space issues, consistent with 'Cambridge planning professionals'.

critic.notes: The summary quote is verbatim and the attribution is correct. However, the stance label 'neutral' is questionable: the planners are expressing a positive belief about rent control's effects, which could be coded as 'in_favor' of rent control. The ambiguity arises because the EIS context is the Kendall Square project, and the planners' view on rent control may be tangential rather than a direct stance on the project itself. Human review is warranted to determine the appropriate stance label given the broader EIS context.

entries.summary_quote: Five people from the audience delivered prepared public comments at the meeting. All were members or people speaking on behalf of the Cambridge Tenants Organizing Committee (CTOC). Remarks included expressions of concern about possible conflict of interest respecting Metcalf & Eddy (M&E); M&E's qualifications to do the study; expansion and development plans by MIT and others in the area surrounding Kendall Square and effect of same on Kendall Square development and adjacent residential areas; the trend toward high intensity development in Cambridge; loss of manufacturing jobs; and, in general, that implementation of "MIT's downtown Boston-type plan" will force more low-income families out of Cambridge.
